prompt: The attached images are for identity reference. Do not be overly influenced by the art style, coloring, line work, proportions, shading, staging, original costume, original pose, body orientation, hand position, leg placement, gaze direction, camera angle, or composition of the reference images. Maintain the facial features, hairstyle, hair color, eyes, perceived age, body type, atmosphere, personality impression, motif, charm, symbolic details, and species. Design a new "Taisho Roman x Modern Fashion" mixed outfit that best suits this character. The goal is not to simply dress them in Taisho-era clothing, but to reimagine what they would wear if they lived in the modern day while naturally incorporating the aesthetics and eclectic atmosphere of the Taisho era. Do not perform a simple transformation of the original outfit; extract hints of colors or motifs only as needed. Aim for a balanced direction of approximately 50% Taisho Roman and 50% Modern Fashion. Use modern clothing as a base while ensuring that the elegance, retro feel, and eclectic Japanese-Western style of the Taisho era are clearly conveyed. However, do not make it a historical reproduction or Japanese cosplay; establish it as sophisticated "Taisho Modern" fashion that can be worn naturally today. The outfit should balance "Taisho Roman elegance, retro feel, and eclectic style" with "modern fashion's sophistication, effortless chic, practicality, and trendiness." Avoid simply adding Japanese patterns to ordinary modern clothes, and conversely, avoid over-reproduction of hakama or kimono. Create a design where both elements blend naturally. Optimize the outfit composition, patterns, color schemes, accessories, and textures by appropriately choosing from styles such as neat, lovely, intellectual, decadent, glamorous, cool, active, androgynous, bewitching, ephemeral, or dignified, according to the character's impression. Permitted elements: Haori-style outerwear, kimono-sleeve details, Japanese coat details, hakama-style bottoms, pleated skirts, long skirts, wide pants, blouses, shirts, dresses, sets, jackets, short tops, standing collars, frills, ribbon ties, obi-style belts, corset-style waist marks, Japanese patterns, floral patterns, geometric patterns, lace, lace-up boots, leather shoes, platforms, pumps, hats, glasses, gloves, clasp bags, mini bags, shoulder bags, umbrellas, hair ornaments, tassels, antique items, and modern accessories. Do not fix the clothing category; choose naturally according to the character's gender impression, build, perceived age, personality, role, and motif. Maintain modern silhouettes, layering, ease of wear, and urban sophistication while clearly reflecting Taisho Roman-derived details and atmosphere. Avoid a cosplay feel and establish it as natural, sophisticated "modern clothing with a Taisho Roman taste." Choose a completely new pose, angle, composition, and expression different from the original image. Using the same standing posture, hand position, facial orientation, center of gravity, or silhouette as the original image is prohibited. Ensure the pose is natural, showcases the outfit well, and conveys the character's personality. Choose a background space that naturally suits the character. Use modern urban spaces, interiors, shops, cafes, or street corners as a base, with a subtle hint of Taisho Roman design or retro atmosphere. The background should not be too prominent and should enhance the character and outfit. Reconstruct the final output into a single unified completed art style. High-quality commercial anime illustration style, sharp lines, clean skin, transparent eyes, well-groomed hair, elegant fabric textures, natural lighting. No text, no logos, no noise.
